      {"heading": "4. MANAGE AI RISKS AND PERFORMANCE (Determining inclusion; Privacy/Civil Liberties/Civil Rights; Managing Performance & Risk; Rights/Safety-Impacting; Rights-Impacting; Generative AI)", "location": "Section 4"},

      {"heading": "6. DEFINITIONS", "location": "Section 6"},
      {"heading": "Appendix I: Promoting Innovative Acquisition", "location": "Appendix I"},

  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c39", "name": "artificial intelligence (AI)", "aliases": ["AI"], "description": "AI technology whose use in the Federal Government presents opportunity and novel risk.", "grounding_span": "The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in the Federal Government presents tremendous", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c1", "name": "responsible acquisition of AI", "aliases": ["responsible AI acquisition"], "description": "The agency capacity, requirements, and practices for acquiring AI responsibly that this memorandum directs agencies to improve.", "grounding_span": "agencies to improve their capacity for the responsible acquisition of AI.", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c2", "name": "AI risk management in acquisition planning", "aliases": ["AI risk management"], "description": "Proactive integration of AI risk considerations into agency acquisition planning.", "grounding_span": "AI risk management into agency acquisition planning", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c3", "name": "rights-impacting AI", "aliases": [], "description": "AI whose agency use impacts rights, triggering M-24-10 risk-management and additional acquisition practices.", "grounding_span": "Rights-Impacting AI: The term “rights-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c4", "name": "safety-impacting AI", "aliases": [], "description": "AI whose agency use impacts safety, triggering M-24-10 risk-management and additional acquisition practices.", "grounding_span": "Safety-Impacting AI: The term “safety-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c5", "name": "generative AI", "aliases": [], "description": "AI that generates content; subject to additional acquisition practices in this memorandum.", "grounding_span": "Additional Practices for Generative AI", "location": "Section 4(f)"},
    {"id": "c29", "name": "general use enterprise-wide generative AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Foundation-model or widely applicable generative AI acquired for general purposes and used across more than one agency component.", "grounding_span": "The term “general use enterprise-wide generative AI” refers in this memorandum to", "location": "Section 4(f)"},
    {"id": "c38", "name": "foundation model", "aliases": [], "description": "A form of widely applicable generative AI system.", "grounding_span": "in the form of a foundation model or other widely applicable generative AI", "location": "Section 4(f)"},
    {"id": "c6", "name": "vendor lock-in", "aliases": [], "description": "Condition where switching costs, licensing restrictions, or poor documentation prevent an agency from changing AI vendors.", "grounding_span": "vendor lock-in", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c7", "name": "interoperability", "aliases": [], "description": "Ability of systems, products, or components to exchange and use information and operate together.", "grounding_span": "interoperability", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c8", "name": "cross-functional collaboration", "aliases": [], "description": "Internal agency collaboration across acquisition, IT, cybersecurity, privacy, and other equities to manage AI performance and risk.", "grounding_span": "Cross-Functional Collaboration", "location": "Section 3(a)(i)"},
    {"id": "c9", "name": "interagency collaboration", "aliases": [], "description": "Collaboration and information sharing on AI acquisition across executive branch agencies.", "grounding_span": "Interagency Collaboration", "location": "Section 3(b)"},
    {"id": "c10", "name": "competitive AI market", "aliases": ["AI marketplace"], "description": "A diverse, competitive, resilient market of AI suppliers that agencies must promote.", "grounding_span": "COMPETITIVE AI MARKET", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "c21", "name": "technical stack", "aliases": [], "description": "The layered set of tasks enabling AI adoption (data collectors/labelers, model developers, hardware/compute providers, integrators, service providers).", "grounding_span": "technical stack", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "c11", "name": "data portability", "aliases": [], "description": "Storing and representing data so it can be reused without conversions or redundant systems.", "grounding_span": "data portability", "location": "Section 5(b)"},
    {"id": "c12", "name": "model portability", "aliases": [], "description": "Storing and representing models so they can be reused across vendors and environments.", "grounding_span": "model portability", "location": "Section 5(b)"},
    {"id": "c13", "name": "performance-based acquisition", "aliases": ["performance-based requirements"], "description": "Acquisition techniques focused on desired performance outcomes to enable proactive risk management.", "grounding_span": "Performance-Based Acquisition Techniques", "location": "Section 4(c)(i)"},
    {"id": "c14", "name": "AI incident reporting", "aliases": [], "description": "Contractual process for identifying and disclosing serious AI incidents and malfunctions to agencies.", "grounding_span": "AI Incident Reporting", "location": "Section 4(d)(v)"},
    {"id": "c15", "name": "AI-based biometric systems", "aliases": ["biometric identification"], "description": "AI systems that identify individuals using biometric identifiers such as faces, irises, fingerprints, or gait.", "grounding_span": "AI-based biometric systems", "location": "Section 4(b)(ii)"},
    {"id": "c16", "name": "confabulations", "aliases": ["hallucinations", "fabrications"], "description": "Confidently stated but erroneous or false content produced by generative AI.", "grounding_span": "confabulations", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c17", "name": "red-teaming", "aliases": [], "description": "Structured adversarial testing of AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Red-teaming", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "c18", "name": "intellectual property rights and ownership", "aliases": ["IP rights"], "description": "Contractual delineation of Government and contractor ownership and IP rights over data, models, and deliverables.", "grounding_span": "Intellectual Property Rights and Ownership", "location": "Section 4(c)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c19", "name": "data management", "aliases": [], "description": "Contractual handling of data as a strategic asset (collection, labeling, storage, retention, use) for acquired AI.", "grounding_span": "Data Management", "location": "Section 4(c)(iv)"},
    {"id": "c20", "name": "environmental impact of AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Energy, carbon, water, hardware, and e-waste impacts of training and using generative AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Mitigate Environmental Impacts", "location": "Section 4(f)(vi)"},
    {"id": "c22", "name": "AI use case inventory", "aliases": [], "description": "Agency inventory of AI use cases that agencies must maintain and annually update.", "grounding_span": "AI use case inventory", "location": "Section 4(a)"},
    {"id": "c23", "name": "privacy risks", "aliases": [], "description": "Privacy risks arising from development, training, acquisition, and use of AI across the acquisition lifecycle.", "grounding_span": "Privacy Risks", "location": "Section 4(b)(i)"},
    {"id": "c24", "name": "algorithmic discrimination", "aliases": ["unlawful bias", "unlawful discrimination"], "description": "Discriminatory outcomes from AI systems that agencies must test for and mitigate.", "grounding_span": "algorithmic discrimination", "location": "Section 4(d)(iii)"},
    {"id": "c25", "name": "content provenance / watermarking", "aliases": [], "description": "Mechanisms such as watermarks and cryptographically-signed metadata that identify and attribute AI-generated outputs.", "grounding_span": "watermarks, cryptographically-signed metadata", "location": "Section 4(f)(i)"},
    {"id": "c26", "name": "open-source alternatives", "aliases": [], "description": "Non-proprietary solutions that agencies must not disadvantage solely due to their non-proprietary nature.", "grounding_span": "open-source alternatives", "location": "Section 4(f)(iv)"},
    {"id": "c27", "name": "market research", "aliases": [], "description": "Pre-acquisition research strategies to understand the AI marketplace and identify vendors including new entrants.", "grounding_span": "Thorough Market Research", "location": "Appendix I(c)"},
    {"id": "c28", "name": "acquisition workforce training", "aliases": ["AI-trained workforce"], "description": "Training so the acquisition workforce can identify, evaluate, and acquire AI effectively.", "grounding_span": "AI-Trained Workforce", "location": "Appendix I(e)"},
    {"id": "c30", "name": "data poisoning", "aliases": [], "description": "Risk unique to AI where training data is corrupted.", "grounding_span": "data poisoning", "location": "Section 4(c)(v)"},
    {"id": "c31", "name": "data leakage", "aliases": [], "description": "Risk unique to AI where sensitive data is exposed through a model.", "grounding_span": "data leakage", "location": "Section 4(c)(v)"},
    {"id": "c32", "name": "data, model, or concept drift", "aliases": ["drift"], "description": "Degradation over time that may require more frequent AI maintenance.", "grounding_span": "data, model, or concept drift", "location": "footnote 27"},
    {"id": "c33", "name": "AI governance", "aliases": [], "description": "Agency responsibilities for governing AI as established by OMB Memorandum M-24-10.", "grounding_span": "AI governance", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c34", "name": "self-preferencing", "aliases": ["anticompetitive practices"], "description": "Anticompetitive behavior such as steering, tying, and preferencing vertically integrated services.", "grounding_span": "self-preferencing across services", "location": "Section 5(f)"},
    {"id": "c35", "name": "knowledge transfer to agency staff", "aliases": [], "description": "Vendor commitment to transfer knowledge and documentation enabling agencies to switch vendors.", "grounding_span": "transfer knowledge to appropriate agency staff", "location": "Section 5(a)(i)"},
    {"id": "c36", "name": "risks unique to AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Additional risks arising from training models with agency data, including data poisoning and data leakage.", "grounding_span": "risks unique to AI", "location": "Section 4(c)(v)"}
  ],

    {"id": "d1", "term": "Agency", "verbatim_text": "The term “agency” has the meaning provided in 44 U.S.C. § 3502(1).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Agency: The term “agency” has the meaning provided in 44 U.S.C. § 3502(1).", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d2", "term": "Artificial Intelligence (AI)", "verbatim_text": "The term “artificial intelligence” has the meaning provided in Section 238(g) of the John S. McCain National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2019.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Artificial Intelligence (AI): The term “artificial intelligence” has the meaning provided in", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d3", "term": "AI Model", "verbatim_text": "The term “AI model” has the meaning provided in Section 3(c) of Executive Order 14110.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "AI Model: The term “AI model” has the meaning provided in Section 3(c) of Executive Order", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d4", "term": "Artificial Intelligence System", "verbatim_text": "The term “artificial intelligence system” has the definition provided in Section 7223 of the Advancing American AI Act.", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Artificial Intelligence System: The term “artificial intelligence system” has the definition", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d5", "term": "Acquisition", "verbatim_text": "The term “acquisition” has the meaning provided in the Federal Acquisition Regulation, subpart 2.1.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Acquisition: The term “acquisition” has the meaning provided in the Federal Acquisition", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d6", "term": "Confabulations", "verbatim_text": "The term “confabulations” has the meaning provided in NIST AI 600-1, which states that the production of confidently stated but erroneous or false content (known colloquially as “hallucinations” or “fabrications”) by which users may be misled or deceived.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Confabulations: The term “confabulations” has the meaning provided in NIST AI 600-1, which", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d7", "term": "Face recognition/face identification", "verbatim_text": "The terms “face recognition” and “face identification” have the meaning provided in NIST’s ANSI/ASTM E2916-19e1 Standard Terminology for Digital and Multimedia Evidence Examination.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Face recognition/face identification: The terms “face recognition” and “face identification” have", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d8", "term": "Federal Information", "verbatim_text": "The term “Federal information” has the meaning provided in OMB Circular A-130.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Federal Information: The term “Federal information” has the meaning provided in OMB Circular", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d9", "term": "Generative AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “generative AI” has the meaning provided in Section 3(p) of Executive Order 14110.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Generative AI: The term “generative AI” has the meaning provided in Section 3(p) of Executive", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d10", "term": "Hyperparameters", "verbatim_text": "The term “hyperparameters” has the meaning provided in the NIST AI RMF glossary.", "normative_status": "standard", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Hyperparameters: The term “hyperparameters” has the meaning provided in the NIST AI RMF", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d11", "term": "National Security System", "verbatim_text": "The term “National Security System” has the meaning provided in 44 U.S.C. § 3552(b)(6).", "normative_status": "statute", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "National Security System: The term “National Security System” has the meaning provided in 44", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d12", "term": "Red-teaming", "verbatim_text": "The term “red-teaming” has the meaning provided in Section 3(d) of Executive Order 14110.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Red-teaming: The term “red-teaming” has the meaning provided in Section 3(d) of Executive", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d13", "term": "Rights-Impacting AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “rights-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB Memorandum M-24-10.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Rights-Impacting AI: The term “rights-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d14", "term": "Risks from the Use of AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “risks from the use of AI” has the meaning provided in OMB Memorandum M-24-10.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Risks from the Use of AI: The term “risks from the use of AI” has the meaning provided in", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d15", "term": "Safety-Impacting AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “safety-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB Memorandum M-24-10.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "Safety-Impacting AI: The term “safety-impacting AI” has the meaning provided in OMB", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "d16", "term": "interoperability", "verbatim_text": "The term “interoperability” generally refers to the ability of two or more systems, products, or components to exchange information and use the information that has been exchanged, including to operate effectively together.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "The term “interoperability” generally refers to the ability of two or more systems, products, or components to", "location": "footnote 3"},
    {"id": "d17", "term": "general use enterprise-wide generative AI", "verbatim_text": "The term “general use enterprise-wide generative AI” refers in this memorandum to generative AI, in the form of a foundation model or other widely applicable generative AI system, that is acquired for general purposes for which the details are infeasible to define prior to procurement.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "The term “general use enterprise-wide generative AI” refers in this memorandum to", "location": "Section 4(f)"},
    {"id": "d18", "term": "derived product", "verbatim_text": "the term “derived product” means any asset created during the process of transforming a data asset (e.g., modifying or enriching through any contractor-performed cleaning, labeling, customization, etc.) for the purposes of training, testing, or validating a model, as well as any AI models, systems, or services developed using a data asset.", "normative_status": "policy", "as_of_date": "2024-09-24", "grounding_span": "the term “derived product” means any asset created during the process of transforming a data asset", "location": "footnote 25"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl1", "claim_text": "This memorandum directs agencies to improve their capacity for the responsible acquisition of AI.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "agencies to improve their capacity for the responsible acquisition of AI.", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl2", "claim_text": "AI poses novel types of risk.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI poses novel types of risk", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl3", "claim_text": "By November 1, 2024, agencies shall identify any contracts associated with agency use of rights-impacting or safety-impacting AI.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "By November 1st , 2024, agencies shall identify any contracts associated with agency use", "location": "Section 2(c)(i)"},
    {"id": "cl4", "claim_text": "No later than December 1, 2024, agencies shall bring identified rights/safety-impacting AI contracts into compliance and ensure new such contracts are consistent with requirements.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "No later than December 1, 2024, agencies shall—", "location": "Section 2(c)(ii)"},
    {"id": "cl5", "claim_text": "A decision to acquire a service or system from a given vendor can influence or limit options for future acquisitions.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "given vendor can influence or limit options for future acquisitions.", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl6", "claim_text": "Agencies must not privilege proprietary solutions over open-source alternatives solely on the basis of their proprietary nature.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "not privilege proprietary solutions over open-source alternatives solely on the", "location": "Section 4(f)(iv)(A)"},
    {"id": "cl7", "claim_text": "It should not be assumed that proprietary solutions are inherently more secure or reliable than open-source alternatives.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "are inherently more secure or reliable than open-source alternatives.", "location": "Section 4(f)(iv)(A)"},
    {"id": "cl8", "claim_text": "Vendors must have a process for disclosing serious AI incidents and malfunctions within 72 hours (or a timely manner based on severity).", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "within 72 hours", "location": "Section 4(d)(v)"},
    {"id": "cl9", "claim_text": "Robust competition can help mitigate some risks associated with AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Robust competition can help mitigate some risks", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "cl10", "claim_text": "The contract should permanently prohibit the use of inputted agency data and outputted results to further train publicly or commercially available AI algorithms.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "The contract should permanently prohibit the use of", "location": "Section 4(c)(iii)(E)"},
    {"id": "cl11", "claim_text": "Agencies must cease use of rights- or safety-impacting AI where required risk management practices cannot be sufficiently implemented.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "services that impact rights or safety in cases where required risk management practices cannot be", "location": "Section 4(d)"},
    {"id": "cl12", "claim_text": "Many AI systems rely on vast amounts of data and have the potential to produce outcomes that result in unlawful discrimination.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "have the potential to produce outcomes that result in unlawful discrimination.", "location": "Section 4(b)(iii)"},
    {"id": "cl13", "claim_text": "AI systems may require more frequent maintenance and triggers for corrective maintenance due to data, model, or concept drift.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI systems may require more frequent maintenance and triggers for conducting corrective maintenance due to", "location": "footnote 27"},
    {"id": "cl14", "claim_text": "In FEVS 2017-2022, the acquisition workforce responded more favorably than the workforce at large about feeling empowered to use good business judgment.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the acquisition workforce has consistently responded more", "location": "footnote 51"},
    {"id": "cl15", "claim_text": "Acquisition of AI systems and services should be treated as a type of IT acquisition for purposes of applying existing guidance.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "acquisition of AI systems and services should be treated as a type of IT", "location": "Section 3(a)"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "i1", "name": "Face Recognition Technology Evaluation and Facial Analytics Technical Evaluation", "owner": "NIST", "year": null, "method": "algorithm and operational testing of facial recognition systems", "grounding_span": "Face Recognition Technology Evaluation", "location": "Section 4(b)(ii)(B)"},
    {"id": "i2", "name": "Federal Employee Viewpoint Surveys (FEVS)", "owner": "U.S. Office of Personnel Management", "year": 2022, "method": "workforce survey", "grounding_span": "Federal Employee Viewpoint", "location": "footnote 51"}
  ],

  ],
